# gocritic: codegenComment

<instructions>
Detects `// Code generated by ...` comments in hand-written files. These comments are conventionally reserved for automatically generated files (e.g., by `stringer`, `mockgen`, `protoc`). Placing them in manually maintained files signals to tooling and developers that the file should not be edited, which is misleading.

Remove the code generation comment from hand-written files. If the file is indeed generated, ensure it is fully generated and not manually edited.
</instructions>

<examples>
## Bad
```go
// Code generated by hand — DO NOT EDIT
package main

func main() {
    // manually written code
}
```

## Good
```go
package main

func main() {
    // manually written code
}
```
</examples>

<patterns>
- `// Code generated` comments in hand-written files
- Partially generated files with codegen headers
- Custom generator comments that don't follow the standard format
- Copy-pasted codegen comments from generated files
</patterns>

<related>
commentedOutCode, deprecatedComment
</related>
